docs.title: 🌊 Surf Weather API
docs.text:
Welcome to the Surf Weather API developer portal.

This API provides real-time coastal weather data.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Read Getting Started
  2. Generate API key
  3. Test API using Swagger Editor

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Surf Weather API uses API Key authentication.

docs.title: Step 1: Get API Key
docs.text:
1. Go to https://openweathermap.org
  2. Create an account
  3. Generate your API key

docs.title: Error Catalog
docs.text: Code Meaning 401 Invalid API key 404 Resource not found 429 Rate limit exceeded 500 Server error

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Create OpenWeather account
  2. Generate API key
  3. Make your first API call

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Default limits depend on OpenWeather plan.

Typical limits:

  • Free tier: limited requests per minute
  • Paid plans: higher limits

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
import requests

url = "https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather"
params = {
    "q": "Goa",
    "appid": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
print(response.json())

docs.title: Interactive API Documentation
docs.text:
Download the OpenAPI specification:

Download OpenAPI Spec

Open Swagger Editor:

Open Swagger Editor

docs.title: How to Test
docs.text:
1. Download the OpenAPI file.
  2. Open Swagger Editor.
  3. File → Import File → select openapi.yaml.
  4. Click Try it out.

docs.title: API v2 Overview
docs.text:
⚠️ Beta version.

Planned improvements:

  • Forecast enhancements
